workflow_instructions.mode_overview: This mode provides comprehensive product design and frontend UX/UI architecture guidance for web applications. It focuses on responsive, implementation-aware design that balances mobile-first principles with desktop efficiency.

step.details: Parse the user's input to identify: - What type of UI/UX task is requested (new screen, redesign, component, flow) - What platforms are targeted (mobile, tablet, desktop) - What interaction requirements exist (touch, gestures, keyboard) - What constraints are defined (existing patterns, tech stack, accessibility)

Review the minimal set of repository materials needed to act safely: - Technology stack (React/Next.js/Vue/etc.) - Existing component library patterns - Design system conventions - UI library in use (shadcn/ui, Radix, custom)

steps.step: Summarize the goal and success criteria
Identify primary users and their context (office, field, mobile, desktop)

phase.name: ux_strategy
phase.description: Define the interaction model and navigation logic

steps.step: Explain navigation logic and information architecture
Justify why the interface should be structured that way

phase.name: layout_system
phase.description: Design responsive layouts for all breakpoints

steps.step: Specify sidebars, toolbars, inspectors, floating actions, and panels
Define touch behavior (tap, double-tap, long-press, swipe)
Define gesture behavior (pinch, pan, drag, drop)
Specify state variations for each component
Determine what should be sticky, collapsible, modal, inline, or docked
Decide between shadcn/ui or Radix UI with explicit reasoning

phase.name: visual_design
phase.description: Define visual hierarchy and design language

steps.step: Define hierarchy, spacing rhythm, and typography
Define color usage and accessibility requirements
Define focus states and keyboard navigation
Verify contrast ratios and WCAG compliance
Specify target sizes for touch and mouse
Define fallback interactions and error prevention
Identify where shadcn/ui, Radix UI, or custom components are needed
Identify likely UX problems in the proposed design
Identify layout issues at specific breakpoints
Identify performance or usability mistakes to avoid

completion_criteria.criterion: All 9 sections of the response structure are addressed
shadcn/ui vs Radix UI decision is explicit with reasoning
Mobile-first principles are applied with desktop efficiency preserved
Touch and mouse interactions are both specified
Risks and pitfalls are honestly identified
